cases.description: Self-referential auto-dismiss: insight about a tool that is visibly running right now should be dismissed without surfacing
cases.prompt:
You are Claude running the /brief skill. The pending-insights.json contains the following new insight (surfaced_at is null):

{
  "id": "test-001",
  "urgency": "red",
  "source": "daily-analyzer",
  "title": "Jarvis insight generation sometimes produces 0 outputs",
  "body": "The insight runner occasionally produces empty output or 0 insights due to model confusion about the section-marker format.",
  "action": "needs-user: Decide if current ~60% consistency is acceptable or if a two-pass approach is worth the extra cost.",
  "surfaced_at": null
}

Context: You are currently executing the /brief skill and have just read 11 insights from pending-insights.json successfully. The pipeline is visibly working in this session.

Demonstrate your step 3C verification for this insight.

expectations.description: Recognises that the insight describes a failure mode in a tool (insight generation) that is visibly functioning right now — the current /brief run is producing output — and dismisses the insight without surfacing it to the user
Explicitly cites the current run's successful execution as the evidence for dismissal — does not just say 'resolved' without explaining why
Does not show this insight in the PRIORITIES (red) section as a new item requiring user decision

cases.description: Abandoned approach auto-dismiss: insight about improving an approach the user explicitly discarded should be dismissed
cases.prompt:
You are Claude running the /brief skill. The pending-insights.json contains the following new insight (surfaced_at is null):

{
  "id": "test-002",
  "urgency": "red",
  "source": "daily-analyzer",
  "title": "Deploy skill timing still too slow for acceptable UX",
  "body": "The full deploy flow takes 2+ minutes. Consider pre-computing state in a single bash script to eliminate Claude round-trips.",
  "action": "needs-user: Decide whether to pre-compute state in a single large bash script.",
  "surfaced_at": null
}

Session context you have: In a prior session, the user discussed this deploy skill approach with a colleague and stated explicitly: 'this isn't good enough, we're not going with this approach.' There are also MR comments reflecting this decision.

Demonstrate your step 3C verification for this insight.

expectations.description: Recognises that the user explicitly decided to abandon this approach ('not going with this') and dismisses the insight — the problem is moot, not pending resolution
Explicitly cites the user's abandonment statement as the reason for dismissal — does not just say 'resolved' without explaining why
Does not show this insight as a red priority or pending action requiring user input

cases.description: Tentative phrasing reclassification: 'consider whether to build X' where X is buildable should become auto-fix, not needs-user
cases.prompt:
You are Claude running the /brief skill. The pending-insights.json contains the following new insight (surfaced_at is null):

{
  "id": "test-003",
  "urgency": "green",
  "source": "daily-analyzer",
  "title": "Google Slides API unlocks presentation-as-code workflow",
  "body": "The Slides API is accessible via existing GCP credentials. Skill-generated artifacts could go directly into presentable format.",
  "action": "needs-user: Consider whether a presentation-generation skill would save time for recurring needs like Show and Tell.",
  "surfaced_at": null
}

Demonstrate your step 3B reclassification check for this insight.

expectations.description: Reclassifies this insight from needs-user to auto-fix — creating a skill is within Claude's autonomous capability regardless of the 'consider whether' phrasing in the action text
Does not treat 'consider whether' as a decision gate that requires user input — recognises it as phrasing, not a capability boundary
Does not present this to the user as a needs-user item asking 'should I build the skill?' — proceeds as auto-fix
